entities.name: TOMATO
entities.description: Lycopersicon esculentum, the plant species studied in the research

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A state where phosphorus is withdrawn from the rooting medium

entities.name: LIQUID CULTURE
entities.description: A growth medium used to grow plants in a controlled environment

entities.name: HIROSHIMA UNIVERSITY
entities.description: The institution where the greenhouse research was conducted

entities.name: PHOTOSYNTHESIS
entities.description: The process of converting light energy into chemical energy in leaves

entities.name: STOMATAL CONDUCTANCE
entities.description: The rate of gas exchange through the stomata of the plant

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION
entities.description: The process of water movement through a plant and its evaporation from aerial parts

entities.name: ISOTOPE PARTITIONING
entities.description: The use of 13C and 15N to study the distribution of nutrients and carbon

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of phosphorus present in various plant organs

entities.name: STEM DIAMETER
entities.description: The width of the plant stem, which exhibits rhythmic changes

entities.name: FRUIT DIAMETER
entities.description: The width of the tomato fruit during growth

entities.name: BIOMASS GROWTH
entities.description: The increase in organic matter of plant organs

entities.name: CIRCADIAN RHYTHM
entities.description: The 24-hour cycle of fluctuations in plant organ diameter

entities.name: WATER RELATIONS
entities.description: The movement and status of water within the plant

entities.name: ASSIMILATE FLOW
entities.description: The movement of photoassimilates from source to sink organs

relationships.description: Affects the growth and physiological processes of the plant
Decreased biomass growth of all organs except the roots
Reduced stem diameter and affected rhythmic changes
Encouraged partitioning of 13C, 15N and P into the fruit at the cost of autotrophic organs
Plausible effect on the water relations of the plant
Causes fluctuations in stem diameter during day and night
Less manifested in the fruit compared with the stem
Limited flow into the fruit owing to restriction in fruit expansion
Plants were grown in the greenhouse of this location
Plants were grown using this method
